nativeRange.habitat: Silty lagoons, harbors, and shallow reef-adjacent areas at depths of 2–30 m, typically found in association with host sea anemones

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied diet of frozen and live mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, chopped table shrimp, and herbivore preparations including spirulina and marine algae. Has been documented consuming both meaty and plant-based foods in the wild.

avoidWith: other Amphiprion polymnus individuals (unless mated pair)

summary: The Black Saddleback Clownfish (Amphiprion polymnus) is a darker color form of the Saddleback Clownfish found across the Western Pacific. Generally considered intermediate in difficulty; tends to be semi-aggressive and benefits from a host anemone.
careNotes:
The Black Saddleback Clownfish is a melanistic (dark) color variant of Amphiprion polymnus, the Saddleback Clownfish. The standard morph is orange-brown with a large white head bar and a distinctive white saddle patch on the back; the black variant replaces the orange-brown areas with deep black. Both color forms are the same species.

Like all clownfish, A. polymnus is protandrous: all individuals are born male, and the dominant individual in a pair becomes female. This is distinct from protogynous hermaphroditism (female-first change). When establishing a pair, introducing both fish simultaneously or purchasing a confirmed mated pair is generally advised to reduce aggression.

This species has been known to be more aggressive toward conspecifics and other clownfish species than Ocellaris or Percula clownfish. It is best kept singly or as a mated pair; aggression toward other clownfish species has been widely documented and is generally considered one of the more territorial clownfish.

Native anemone hosts in the wild include the Sebae Anemone (Heteractis crispa) and the Carpet Anemone (Stichodactyla haddoni); in aquaria, Ritteri Anemone (Heteractis magnifica) and Carpet Anemone (Stichodactyla sp.) are also accepted. An anemone is not strictly required for captive survival, though its presence has been reported to reduce stress and may encourage breeding behavior.

Wild habitat (per FishBase) tends toward silty lagoons and harbors rather than pristine reefs — this species may be somewhat more tolerant of moderate nutrient levels than strict SPS-reef inhabitants.

Water parameter notes: Temperature recommendations varied meaningfully across sources. LiveAquaria lists 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), BulkReefSupply 75–80°F (23.9–26.7°C), FishBase environmental model 25.4–29.3°C, and Reeflings 24–28°C. Averaged values (min 24°C, max 27.5°C) were used. pH recommendations ranged from 7.7–8.5 (BulkReefSupply, wider range) to 8.1–8.4 (LiveAquaria, Reeflings); averaged to 8.0–8.4. Salinity ranged from SG 1.020–1.026 across sources; averaged to SG 1.021–1.026. Tank minimum: LiveAquaria and BulkReefSupply cite 30 gallons (~114 L) while Reeflings suggests 100 L; averaged to ~110 L.

Adult size: FishBase records a wild maximum of 13.0 cm; LiveAquaria lists a 4-inch (10.16 cm) maximum; Reeflings gives 12 cm. These are consistent; the 11.7 cm average max was used. Most aquarium specimens are reported in the 8–12 cm range at maturity.
breedingNotes: Amphiprion polymnus has been reported to breed in captivity, though it is generally considered somewhat more challenging to spawn and rear than the more commonly kept Ocellaris and Percula clownfish. Females tend to be the larger of a pair. Egg-laying occurs on a flat, clean surface near the host anemone or another chosen site; both parents participate in guarding and aerating the clutch. Eggs have been reported to hatch in approximately 8–11 days depending on water temperature. Larval rearing is the primary challenge: newly hatched fry require dedicated rearing tanks and an initial diet of rotifers, transitioning to baby brine shrimp as they develop.

primary.notes: Direct product page for Black Saddleback Clownfish. Provided adult size (4 in / 10.16 cm), temperature (72–78°F / 22.2–25.6°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (SG 1.020–1.025), minimum tank (30 gal / ~114 L), care level (Moderate), temperament (Semi-Aggressive), diet (Omnivore), reef safe (yes), and breeding notes.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (13.0 cm wild max), temperature range (25.4–29.3°C env. model), taxonomy (Pomacentridae, Ovalentaria), native range (Western Pacific: Ryukyu Islands, China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Gulf of Thailand, Indonesia, Philippines, Australia, New Guinea, Solomon Islands), diet (omnivore, trophic level 2.7), IUCN status (Least Concern). Confirmed protandrous hermaphroditism.

Used to inform parameter averaging.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs have been reported to spawn on flat surfaces near a host anemone in captivity; larval rearing is considered more demanding than for Ocellaris or Percula clownfish, requiring dedicated rearing tanks and rotifers as first food.
